question_text: A book is resting on a table. The weight of the book is 20 N downwards. Which statement correctly describes the forces acting on the book?

options.label: A
options.text: Only the weight force of 20 N acts on the book

options.label: B
options.text: The normal force from the table is 20 N upwards, balancing the weight

options.label: C
options.text: The normal force is less than 20 N because the book is at rest

options.label: D
options.text: There is no normal force because the book is not moving

correct_answer: The normal force from the table is 20 N upwards, balancing the weight
explanation: When an object is at rest on a surface, the forces acting on it are balanced. The weight of the book (20 N downwards) is balanced by the normal force from the table (20 N upwards). These equal and opposite forces mean the net force is zero, so the book remains stationary.
